content.text: AutoGen 0.4+ ships as two separate packages:

(the low-level building blocks). AutoGen Core provides the message-passing runtime, agent base classes, subscriptions, and topics that AgentChat is built on. Understanding Core lets you build custom agent runtimes, distributed deployments, and agent patterns that AgentChat does not directly expose.

attrs.alt: AutoGen architecture: autogen-agentchat (high-level) builds on autogen-core (BaseAgent, AgentRuntime, topics), which runs on SingleThreadedAgentRuntime or DistributedAgentRuntime

content.text: AutoGen Core is a separate pip package from the AgentChat layer. Install only what you need:

# Core only (low-level primitives, no AgentChat helpers)
pip install autogen-core

# AgentChat on top of Core (includes autogen-core as a dependency)
pip install autogen-agentchat

# With OpenAI model support
pip install autogen-agentchat autogen-ext[openai]
Abstract base class for all agents — implement handle_message() to define behaviour
Executes agents and routes messages; two implementations: SingleThreadedAgentRuntime and DistributedAgentRuntime
Named channel used for publish/subscribe message routing
Declares which topics an agent listens to
Metadata attached to every message: sender, topic, cancellation token
Unique identity of an agent instance within a runtime
Defining a Custom Agent with BaseAgent
to define how your agent responds to messages. Use the
decorator to route specific message types to dedicated methods.

is the local in-process runtime. It runs all agents in a single async event loop — suitable for development, testing, and single-machine deployments. Register agents by type, then send messages to them by

import asyncio
from autogen_core import SingleThreadedAgentRuntime, AgentId

async def main():
    runtime = SingleThreadedAgentRuntime()

    # Register the agent type; runtime creates instances on demand
    await SummarizerAgent.register(
        runtime,
        type="summarizer",
        factory=lambda: SummarizerAgent("summarizer", runtime),
    )

    runtime.start()

    # Send a message to a specific agent instance
    result = await runtime.send_message(
        TextMessage(content="Hello from the runtime!"),
        AgentId(type="summarizer", key="default"),
    )
    print(result.content)

    await runtime.stop_when_idle()

asyncio.run(main())
AutoGen Core supports pub/sub messaging via
. Agents subscribe to topics and receive all messages published to those topics. This decouples senders from receivers — the publisher does not need to know which agents are listening.
Core vs AgentChat: When to Use Each
Yes — BaseAgent handles any message type
Yes — inject messages directly into the runtime

start with AgentChat for LLM-driven conversations. Drop down to Core when you need custom message types, non-LLM agents, distributed deployment across machines, or fine-grained control over message routing that GroupChat does not support.
Every message in AutoGen Core carries a
. Agents can check the token during long operations and stop early if the caller cancelled the request. This enables cooperative cancellation without killing threads.
AutoGen Core supports two message delivery patterns. Direct messaging via
and returns a reply. Pub/sub via
broadcasts to all agents subscribed to a
without returning a reply. Use direct messaging for request-reply and pub/sub for event broadcasting.

import asyncio
from autogen_core import SingleThreadedAgentRuntime, AgentId, TopicId

async def demo():
    runtime = SingleThreadedAgentRuntime()
    # ... register agents ...
    runtime.start()

    # Direct: send to a specific agent and await its reply
    reply = await runtime.send_message(
        TextMessage(content="What is 2 + 2?"),
        AgentId(type="calculator", key="default"),
    )
    print(f"Direct reply: {reply.content}")

    # Pub/sub: publish an event -- no reply, all subscribers receive it
    await runtime.publish_message(
        NewsItem(headline="New deployment complete"),
        topic_id=TopicId(type="system-events", source="ci"),
    )

    await runtime.stop_when_idle()

asyncio.run(demo())
with isinstance checks. Each decorated method handles one message type, making routing explicit and readable.
the runtime uses the type annotations on
methods to build the dispatch table. If a message type has no matching handler, the runtime raises
Agents can save internal state with
. This enables checkpointing long conversations and resuming after a process restart.
Test Core agents by creating a runtime, registering the agent under test, sending messages directly, and asserting on replies. No mocking is needed for pure message-passing logic.

import asyncio
from autogen_core import SingleThreadedAgentRuntime, AgentId

async def test_summarizer_returns_text():
    runtime = SingleThreadedAgentRuntime()
    await SummarizerAgent.register(runtime, type="summarizer",
        factory=lambda: SummarizerAgent("summarizer", runtime))
    runtime.start()

    reply = await runtime.send_message(
        TextMessage(content="AutoGen Core provides a low-level runtime."),
        AgentId(type="summarizer", key="default"),
    )

    assert isinstance(reply, TextMessage)
    assert "Received:" in reply.content

    await runtime.stop_when_idle()

asyncio.run(test_summarizer_returns_text())
print("Test passed.")
For multi-machine deployments, AutoGen Core provides
which routes messages over gRPC. Each machine runs a runtime worker connecting to a central broker. Agents on different machines communicate via the same
API — the distribution is transparent to agent code.

# Start the broker:
# python -m autogen_core.distributed.worker_gateway --port 50051

# On each worker machine:
from autogen_core.distributed import WorkerAgentRuntime

async def start_worker():
    runtime = WorkerAgentRuntime(host_address="localhost:50051")
    await SummarizerAgent.register(runtime, type="summarizer",
        factory=lambda: SummarizerAgent("summarizer", runtime))
    await runtime.start()
    await runtime.stop_when_idle()

# asyncio.run(start_worker())
handles most use cases including GroupChat and nested chats. Switch to
only when agents must run on separate machines for scale, fault tolerance, or resource isolation.

AutoGen Core supports interceptors that run before and after message delivery. Use them to add logging, metrics, tracing, or rate limiting without modifying individual agent code. Register an interceptor on the runtime and it applies to all messages.
Interceptors receive the message before it is dispatched to handlers.
Call super().on_send() / super().on_publish() to continue the chain — interceptors are composable.
Common uses: structured logging, OpenTelemetry tracing, rate limiting, input validation.
AutoGen Core (autogen-core) is the low-level package providing BaseAgent, AgentRuntime, TopicId, Subscription, and MessageContext.
autogen-agentchat (AssistantAgent, GroupChat, etc.) is a high-level layer built on top of autogen-core.
Implement BaseAgent.handle_message() to define custom agent behaviour for any message type.
SingleThreadedAgentRuntime runs all agents in a single async event loop; DistributedAgentRuntime spans multiple machines via gRPC.
Publish-subscribe via TopicId and TypeSubscription decouples publishers from subscribers.
Use AgentChat for standard LLM conversations; use Core for custom routing, non-LLM agents, or distributed deployments.
CancellationToken in MessageContext enables cooperative cancellation of long-running agent tasks.
